original_image_tags: Grainy, monochrome infrared sensor still, likely from an aircraft targeting pod. The image is in Black Hot (BHOT) polarity, where heat sources appear dark against a lighter, cooler sky. A white crosshair (tracking reticle) is centered in the frame. Just below and to the right of the crosshair's center is a distinct, elongated, dark object. The object appears to be two connected or closely adjacent dark masses, possibly indicating two heat sources or a single object with a complex shape. The background is a uniform, noisy gray, suggesting a clear sky with sensor noise. Several rectangular black bars (redactions) obscure text overlays in the top, left, and right portions of the image. In the bottom left corner, white text reads: "12/31/99 18:19:54". The horizontal line of the crosshair has small tick marks at its ends, each labeled with the number "3".

elements.description: Grayscale thermal or infrared photograph showing one small, dark, irregularly shaped object slightly right of center against a noisy gray background. A white crosshair reticle is centered in the frame with small '3' markings on the horizontal axis. Timestamp at bottom left reads '12/31/99 18:19:54'.
Multiple large black rectangular redaction blocks covering text fields at the top, left, and right sides of the image.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual elements (thermal/infrared image, single dark object right of center, centered white crosshair with '3' markings, bottom-left timestamp, and black redaction blocks), but Description A adds specific details (e.g., 'elongated' and 'two connected or closely adjacent dark masses') that Description B does not mention.
